steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with an appositive phrase describing a medal recipient. The appositive must be immediately followed by the noun it names — that noun must be a person.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the result of chemist Ahmed Zewail's expansion of the limits of measurability was the introduction of a new scientific discipline: Appositives must rename a person here; "results" cannot be a medal recipient. ✗
  • Choice B — the introduction of a new scientific discipline by chemist Ahmed Zewail led to the expansion of the limits of measurability: Same violation. ✗
  • Choice C — the limits of measurability were expanded by chemist Ahmed Zewail who introduced a new scientific discipline: Same violation. ✗
  • Choice D — chemist Ahmed Zewail expanded the limits of measurability and introduced a new scientific discipline: A person immediately follows the appositive, satisfying the grammatical requirement. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
